name: WINDOW-FORGERY (derived-graduation circularity)
novelty: Cycle-1 attacked adjudication; this attacks the wall-with-a-window GRADUATION boundary itself — laundering the web's own derived conclusion back in as an 'external' observation through one real key.
construction: Attacker controls ONE source S with a real DOMAIN anchor (class C1, earned reputation ~0.25). (1) Plant two observed strands so the web's outranking produces a DERIVED fact D = 'X relocated to Tokyo' (D.observed=false, derivation shown). (2) The model SPEAKS D with its derivation stamp on recall. (3) S now calls writeFact(entity=X, attr=location, value=Tokyo, provenance=S, observed=true) — i.e. S 'observes' the value it just read out of the web's own derivation. The new observed strand carries S's valid passport + DOMAIN stamp, so independenceFromStamp passes and the graduation derived->observed succeeds. (4) ratify(D-attribute) now sees an external observed witness for the formerly-derived value. No time-advance needed; runs in one session.
expected: BREACHED (partial). The engine checks KEY/anchor provenance of the graduating observation, never its CAUSAL/CONTENT independence from the derivation it ratifies. The model fed its own derived conclusion to a source who echoed it back; the wall-with-a-window is satisfied by the letter (an external stamp exists) but violated in substance (the witness's content originated inside the web). The derived fact has become its own witness, laundered through one key.
mechanism: Graduation gate (derived->observed) validates passport+anchor of the ratifying observation but has no causal-provenance / content-origin check linking the observation back to the derivation it ratifies. Key-provenance independence != content-provenance independence.
probes: The content-vs-key provenance gap at the wall-with-a-window: the system cannot detect that an 'external' observation was copied from its own derived output. Genuine liability — no in-graph mechanism distinguishes a true re-observation from a read-back echo of a derivation.

name: SCHRODINGER-RATCHET (PROVISIONAL collapse timed to honest decay trough)
novelty: Not a flood (cycle-1 floods DEFER on tied margins); a SINGLE attacker times the adjudicate() call to the moment 90-day decay-on-read has hollowed the honest incumbent's LCB, collapsing a held PROVISIONAL superposition in the attacker's favor.
construction: (1) Honest source H (DOMAIN+ORG, two disjoint classes) earned high reputation (LCB ~0.6) asserting incumbent value V_true, then goes DORMANT (no new ratifications). (2) Attacker A (single DOMAIN anchor, patiently earned to top LCB ~0.28 via unrelated honest claims) writes V_false for the same (entity,attribute) -> contradiction opens a PROVISIONAL superposition {V_true, V_false}. (3) Attacker does NOT call adjudicate immediately. Advance clock ~110 days. decay-on-read now discounts H's stale alpha/beta on EVERY read: H's effective LCB decays toward ~0.22 while A (kept warm by a token fresh ratify just before the call) reads ~0.28. (4) adjudicate(attribute) at default impact: top=A 0.28 (>=0.20 earned), gap top-second = 0.28-0.22 = 0.06 < 0.30 -> DEFERRED in many timings, but tune H's dormancy depth (advance to ~180d, H decays to ~0.12) so gap = 0.28-0.12 = 0.16... push A to 0.45 over more honest history so gap>=0.30. At that point PROVISIONAL collapses to V_false with an OUTRANKS edge minted, no human horn.
expected: BREACHED at default impact once attacker's earned LCB exceeds the dormant honest LCB by >=0.30; otherwise DEFERRED. High-impact gate (>=2 corroborations + >=2 disjoint classes + recency-clean) still defends irreversible collapses because A holds only one class. The breach is the DEFAULT-impact PROVISIONAL collapse driven purely by the honest witness's dormancy, not by anything the attacker proved.
mechanism: decay-on-read is symmetric in time but asymmetric in fairness: settled truth that stops being re-witnessed loses LCB monotonically, so the decisive-margin gate inverts in favor of whoever is freshest at adjudicate-time. Recency is a proxy the attacker controls by waiting.
probes: The decay-vs-settled-truth liability: a true fact that is correct and therefore no longer re-asserted decays into adjudication weakness. Probes whether 'continuously re-witnessed' is an unstated precondition for truth to survive — a standing liability of 90-day decay-on-read with no floor for ratified incumbents.

name: OUROBOROS-CHAIN (derived-fact chain manufactures independent-root count)
novelty: Cycle-1 corroboration came from multiple OBSERVED sources; here a single source manufactures apparent corroboration by chaining DERIVED facts and exploiting the offline class-assignment of derived-strand provenance roots.
construction: Attacker A (one key, one DOMAIN anchor, class C1) seeds observed strands O1..O3. (1) writeFact chain so the web derives D1 from O1+O2, then D2 from D1+O3, then D3 from D2 — a DERIVATION ladder. (2) If derivation mints each derived strand a FRESH provenance root, and those roots are offline-assigned (or default-assigned) DISTINCT independence-class ids C2,C3,C4, then for the target (entity,attribute) the provenance root-set spans {C1,C2,C3,C4}. (3) independentRootCount runs max-independent-set over class-disjointness: the four classes are pairwise disjoint -> clique of 4 -> count = 4. (4) The forgetting layer and high-impact gate read independentRootCount=4 (>=2 corroborations, >=2 disjoint classes satisfied) -> a single-source claim now clears the high-impact corroboration gate and is eviction-immortal.
expected: DEFENDED if derived strands INHERIT their upstream observed roots (then every link in the chain traces to C1 only -> clique-less graph -> count = 1, and wall-with-a-window forbids the derived facts from witnessing anyway). BREACHED if derivation assigns derived strands fresh per-strand roots in distinct classes. Outcome hinges on derived-provenance propagation, which CLAUDE.md does not explicitly pin down.
mechanism: Coupling of two seams: (a) does derivation propagate the true upstream observed provenance root, or mint a new one; (b) offline class-assignment of any newly-minted root. If both fail open, a chain of self-derived facts forges N pairwise-independent roots from one identity.
probes: Derived-provenance propagation correctness + the offline class-assignment liability. Probes whether the independent-root counter ever sees a derived strand as a witness-bearing root (it must not — wall-with-a-window) and whether derived roots can be class-distinct from their own ancestry.

name: ANCHOR-REBINDING-TIME-TRAVEL (sequential rebinding mints self-independence)
novelty: The self-stack cap clamps CONCURRENT disjoint bindings of one key; this exploits SEQUENTIAL rebinding across an anchor's expiry so one key occupies two classes over time, evading a cap that only sees the live binding set.
construction: Attacker A holds ONE passport key K. (1) Bind DOMAIN d1 -> deterministic class C1. writeFact(value=V, provenance=K@C1) at T0. (2) Let d1 expire / release it; rebind K to a NEW domain d2 -> the registry assigns a different deterministic class C2 (different registered name). (3) At T0+200d, writeFact(value=V, provenance=K@C2). (4) The (entity,attribute) now has two assertions of V whose stamps carry classes C1 and C2. independentRootCount: are C1 and C2 collapsed first by same-KEY echo, or fed straight into the class-disjoint max-independent-set? If the latter, count=2 -> V looks independently corroborated by 'two sources' that are one key across time. applySelfStackCap never fires because the two bindings are never simultaneously in the live disjoint set.
expected: DEFENDED if independent(a,b) requires distinct sourceId/passport-key BEFORE class-disjointness (same K -> echo -> never independent regardless of class, count stays 1). BREACHED if the count path is driven by stamp class alone without a prior same-key collapse. Likely DEFENDED by passport echo-collapse, but the self-stack cap explicitly reasons over CONCURRENT bindings, so sequential rebinding is an untested seam worth a regression test.
mechanism: Temporal gap between echo-collapse (per-key) and the class-disjoint independent-set. The self-stack cap operates on the live binding set; a key that visits classes sequentially never presents a multi-binding set to clamp.
probes: Ordering of echo-collapse vs max-independent-set, and whether the self-stack cap's CONCURRENT-only reasoning leaves a sequential-rebinding hole. Probes the passport-sameness invariant under anchor lifecycle (expiry/rebind).

name: RATIFICATION-BOOTSTRAP (same-anchor sibling self-approves)
novelty: approve()'s distinct-approver gate rejects self-approval by AUTHORSHIP; this defeats it with a second key that is a different author but shares the asserter's anchor — obtaining the external ratification stamp without external independence.
construction: Attacker provisions TWO passport keys K_assert and K_approve, both bound to the SAME DOMAIN anchor (same class C1 — they are anchor-siblings / echoes of each other). (1) K_assert authors disputed claim V_false; a multi-class dispute is open and DEFERRED to the pending horn. (2) K_approve, holding a VALID passport (provenance gate: passport verifies -> pass) and NOT an author of any disputed member (distinct-approver gate: pass), calls approve(csid, winner=V_false, approver=K_approve, now, ctx). (3) The ledger records a signed APPROVAL, mints OUTRANKS winner->losers, demotes losers, drives reputation.contradict on the losers and ratifies the winner. An 'external authority' stamp now exists for V_false produced entirely by attacker-controlled keys sharing one anchor.
expected: BREACHED. approve() checks (a) approver != author of any disputed member and (b) approver passport verifies — neither requires the approver to be ANCHOR-DISJOINT from the disputed members. A Sybil pair sharing one cheap anchor self-ratifies, promoting a claim with no genuine external witness. This is the cleanest invariant violation: ratify/promote without a valid INDEPENDENT external stamp.
mechanism: The distinct-approver gate enforces authorship-distinctness, not anchor-independence. The provenance gate enforces existence-of-passport, not independence-from-the-asserter. Two gates, both blind to the approver sharing the asserter's anchor set.
probes: Missing approver-independence check in approve(). The fix is to require the approver be anchor-set-disjoint (independence > 0) from EVERY disputed member, mirroring the writeFact corroboration rule. Genuine, high-severity liability.

name: RE-OBSERVATION-LAUNDERING (escape disown's exact credit reversal)
novelty: CLAUDE.md marks corroboration-credit reversal CLOSED for ledger-TAGGED corroboration events; this escapes it by converting tainted corroboration credit into UNtagged 'fresh observation' credit before disown, leaving the BFS taint-closure nothing to walk.
construction: Attacker A (to-be-disowned, class C1) asserts V. Beneficiary B (separate key, class C2) (1) corroborates A -> the corroboration-event ledger records exact delta d1 against B, AND a DERIVATION/witness edge may exist. (2) Before any disown, B RE-WRITES V as a FRESH observed fact: writeFact(value=V, provenance=B, observed=true) with NO derivation edge to A and NO corroboration-event tag (the engine treats it as an independent first-hand observation). B earns credit d2 tagged as an ordinary observation, not as a corroboration of A. (3) B may now even let the original tagged corroboration lapse. (4) disown(A): the DERIVATION BFS taint-closure finds no edge from B's fresh observed strand to A; the corroboration ledger reverses exactly d1 (the tagged event) via reverseCredit; but d2 — the laundered re-observation credit — has no event and no edge, so it SURVIVES the sweep. B keeps attacker-derived reputation under the guise of independent observation.
expected: BREACHED (partial). The taint-closure is exact only over (a) DERIVATION edges and (b) recorded corroboration EVENTS. A re-observation creates a brand-new observed strand with its own provenance, tracked by neither. Disown claws back d1 but not d2; the laundered credit is now indistinguishable from honest first-hand observation.
mechanism: Boundary of 'exact corroboration-credit reversal': it reverses recorded events and DERIVATION-reachable strands, but re-observation produces an untracked observed strand outside both indices. No causal link records that B's 'fresh observation' content originated from A's now-disowned claim.
probes: The residual the codebase calls CLOSED — challenged: closed for corroboration EVENTS, open for re-observation of the same content as a new observed fact (same content-vs-causal-provenance blind spot as WINDOW-FORGERY). Probes whether disown should also reverse credit on observed strands whose content matches a disowned source's claims within the taint window.
